_meta.coverage_note: FULL CORPUS — all 30 June dates. June contains the Nativity of John the Baptist (Jun 24, rank 1 Great Feast) and the Feast of the Holy Apostles Peter and Paul (Jun 29, rank 1). The Apostles' Fast precedes the Peter-Paul feast. Pentecost and Trinity Sunday may fall in June; Paschal feast-rank overrides deferred per governance. Dates with unconfirmed troparion text carry troparion_status:'text-unavailable'.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Justin the Philosopher

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Justin the Philosopher
troparion.text: Having known the truth through the study of wisdom, and having declared Christ before His enemies, thou didst seal thy witness with thy blood, O Justin the Philosopher. With bold confidence, O holy martyr, intercede for us before Christ our God.

commemorations.notes: Justin Martyr (+ca. 165), Christian apologist and philosopher, martyred in Rome. Rank 4.

commemorations.name: Translation of the Relics of St. Nicephorus, Patriarch of Constantinople

troparion.title: Troparion of the Translation of the Relics of St. Nicephorus of Constantinople
troparion.text: Through your inspired confession, you gained victory for the Church, holy Hierarch Nicephorus. You suffered unjust exile because of your reverence for the icon of God the Word. Righteous Father, entreat Christ our God to grant us His great mercy.

commemorations.notes: Translation of the relics of Nicephorus I of Constantinople (+828), confessor under Iconoclasm. Main feast June 2; translation of relics from Prokonnis to Constantinople (846). Rank 4. Troparion Tone 3 confirmed: OCA (Jun. 2); corroborated by Antiochian. Tone corrected from 4 to 3.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Lucillian and companions

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Lucillian and companions
troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Lucillian and companions, O Lord, through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through their intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Lucillian and four young companions (+273), martyrs in Byzantium. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, plural form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: St. Metrophanes, First Patriarch of Constantinople

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Metrophanes, First Patriarch of Constantinople
troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence. Your humility exalted you; your poverty enriched you. Hierarch Father Metrophanes, entreat Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: Metrophanes of Constantinople (+325), first patriarch. Rank 4. Standard hierarch troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Hieromartyr Dorotheus, Bishop of Tyre

troparion.title: Troparion of the Hieromartyr Dorotheus, Bishop of Tyre
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Dorotheus, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Dorotheus of Tyre (+362), bishop and martyr under Julian the Apostate. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Venerable Bessarion of Egypt
commemorations.notes: Hold — monastic type. Troparion text not securely confirmed.

commemorations.name: Hieromartyr Marcellus, Bishop of Rome

troparion.title: Troparion of the Hieromartyr Marcellus, Bishop of Rome
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Marcellus, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Marcellus I, Bishop of Rome (+309), martyr. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Translation of the Relics of the Great Martyr Theodore Stratelates

troparion.title: Troparion of Theodore Stratelates (Translation of Relics)
troparion.text: In thy sufferings, O Theodore Stratelates, thou didst receive an incorruptible crown from Christ God; as thou art adorned with thy blood, thou hast put to shame the false gods. By thy prayers deliver us from every adversity, O most lauded one.

commemorations.notes: Translation of relics of Theodore Stratelates (+319). Uses the same troparion as his primary feast (Feb 8, Tone 4). Rank 4.

commemorations.name: St. Cyril, Archbishop of Alexandria

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Cyril of Alexandria
troparion.text: As a guide of Orthodoxy, a teacher of piety and modesty, a luminary of the world and an adornment of hierarchs, thou hast enlightened all by thy teachings, O holy Cyril. Intercede with Christ our God to save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Cyril of Alexandria (+444), Doctor of the Church, champion of Theotokos title at Ephesus (431). Rank 3. Also commemorated Jan 18 together with Athanasius.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyrs Alexander and Antonina

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyrs Alexander and Antonina
troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Alexander and Antonina, O Lord, through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through their intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Alexander and Antonina (+4th c.), martyrs of Caesarea in Cappadocia. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, plural masculine form applied (mixed group). No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Apostles Bartholomew and Barnabas

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostles Bartholomew and Barnabas
troparion.text: O Holy Apostles Bartholomew and Barnabas, intercede with the merciful God, that He grant our souls forgiveness of offences.

commemorations.notes: Bartholomew (one of the Twelve) and Barnabas (companion of Paul, Acts 14). Joint feast Jun 11. Rank 3. Standard apostle troparion form (Tone 3).

commemorations.name: Venerable Onuphrius the Great

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Onuphrius the Great
troparion.text: Earnestly desiring the life of the angels, O Onuphrius, thou didst clothe thyself in angelic virtue while still in the flesh; for having quenched the flame of the passions with the streams of compunction, thou hast bloomed in the desert as a fruitful tree, a man of God always abounding in virtues. Intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: Onuphrius the Great (+4th c.), naked ascetic of the Egyptian desert. Rank 3.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Aquilina of Byblos

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Aquilina of Byblos
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Aquilina, O Lord, through her sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, she laid low her adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through her intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Aquilina of Byblos (+293), child martyr under Diocletian. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, feminine form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Prophet Elisha

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Prophet Elisha
troparion.text: We celebrate the memory of Thy prophet Elisha, O Lord, and through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Elisha the Prophet (9th c. BC), successor of Elijah. Rank 4. Standard prophet troparion form.

commemorations.name: Blessed Augustine, Bishop of Hippo

troparion.title: Troparion of Blessed Augustine, Bishop of Hippo
troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence. Your humility exalted you; your poverty enriched you. Hierarch Father Augustine, entreat Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: Augustine of Hippo (+430), Doctor of the Western Church, venerated in the Byzantine Menaion on Jun 15. Rank 4. Standard hierarch troparion Tone 4 applied. No confirmed Eastern unique troparion text at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: St. Tikhon, Bishop of Amathous in Cyprus

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Tikhon, Bishop of Amathous in Cyprus
troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence. Your humility exalted you; your poverty enriched you. Hierarch Father Tikhon, entreat Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: Tikhon of Amathous (+5th c.), bishop and wonder-worker of Cyprus. Rank 4. Standard hierarch troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyrs Manuel, Sabel, and Ismael

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyrs Manuel, Sabel, and Ismael
troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Manuel, Sabel, and Ismael, O Lord, through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through their intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Manuel, Sabel, and Ismael (+362), Persian martyrs under Julian the Apostate. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, plural form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Leontius of Phoenicia

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Leontius of Phoenicia
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Leontius, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Leontius of Phoenicia (+2nd c.), soldier-martyr. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Apostle Jude, Brother of the Lord

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostle Jude, Brother of the Lord
troparion.text: O Apostle Jude, relative of Christ our God and pillar of the Church, intercede with the Master of all that He grant peace to all the world and to our souls great mercy.

commemorations.notes: Jude, Brother of the Lord (Thaddaeus), author of the Epistle of Jude. Rank 3.

commemorations.name: Hieromartyr Methodius, Bishop of Patara

troparion.title: Troparion of the Hieromartyr Methodius, Bishop of Patara
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Methodius, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Methodius of Olympus (+ca. 311), bishop and theologian. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Julian of Tarsus

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Julian of Tarsus
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Julian, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Julian of Tarsus (+3rd c.), young martyr. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Hieromartyr Eusebius, Bishop of Samosata

troparion.title: Troparion of the Hieromartyr Eusebius of Samosata
troparion.text: Thou didst share in the ways of the apostles and occupy their throne; thou didst find the practice of virtue to be a passage to divine vision. Wherefore, having rightly divided the word of truth, thou didst also pour out thy blood for the faith, O Hieromartyr Eusebius. Intercede with Christ God to save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Eusebius of Samosata (+380), bishop, defender of Nicene faith, martyred by Arian. Rank 3.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Agrippina of Rome

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Agrippina of Rome
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Agrippina, O Lord, through her sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, she laid low her adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through her intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Agrippina of Rome (+3rd c.), virgin martyr. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, feminine form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: The Nativity of the Holy Glorious Prophet, Forerunner, and Baptist John

troparion.title: Troparion of the Nativity of John the Baptist
troparion.text: The Prophet and Forerunner of the coming of Christ, we worthily honor thee with love, for the barrenness of her that bore thee was loosed by the divine grace, and the dumbness of the father was freed by thy divine birth, O Baptist John, and the Incarnation of the Son of God is proclaimed to the world.

commemorations.notes: Great Feast — Nativity of John the Baptist. Rank 1.

commemorations.name: Venerable Martyr Febronia of Nisibis

troparion.title: Troparion of the Venerable Martyr Febronia of Nisibis
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Febronia, O Lord, through her sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, she laid low her adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through her intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Febronia of Nisibis (+ca. 304), nun and martyr under Diocletian. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, feminine form applied. Type is 'martyr' in batch; promoted accordingly. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Venerable David of Thessalonica
commemorations.notes: Hold — monastic type. Troparion text not securely confirmed.

commemorations.name: Venerable Sampson the Hospitable
commemorations.notes: Hold — type 'saint' is not in the auto-promote list. Troparion text not securely confirmed.

commemorations.name: Forefeast of the Feast of the Holy Apostles Peter and Paul

troparion.title: Troparion of the Forefeast of Peter and Paul
troparion.text: O Chief Apostles Peter and Paul, ye are the foundations of the Church and its teachers; through your intercessions establish her in peace, O Only Lover of mankind.

commemorations.notes: Forefeast of the Feast of Sts. Peter and Paul (Jun 29). Uses the apostles' troparion. Rank 4.

commemorations.name: The Holy Glorious and All-Praised Chief Apostles Peter and Paul

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Chief Apostles Peter and Paul
troparion.text: O Chief Apostles Peter and Paul, ye are the foundations of the Church and its teachers; through your intercessions establish her in peace, O Only Lover of mankind.

commemorations.notes: Feast of the Holy Apostles Peter and Paul. Rank 1 (Great Feast level in many calendars; technically ranked as Great Feast or rank 2 depending on typikon — entered here as rank 1 per OCA usage). End of the Apostles' Fast.

commemorations.name: Synaxis of the Holy Glorious and All-Praised Twelve Apostles

troparion.title: Troparion of the Synaxis of the Twelve Apostles
troparion.text: O holy Apostles, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls forgiveness of transgressions.

commemorations.notes: Synaxis of the Holy Twelve Apostles — the day after the Peter-Paul feast. Rank 3.
